differential_diagnoses: Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) associated with PASH syndrome (pyoderma gangrenosum, acne, and hidradenitis suppurativa)
Crohn's disease with extraintestinal manifestations (metastatic Crohn's)
Vasculitis (e.g., polyarteritis nodosa or ANCA-associated vasculitis)
Pyogenic arthritis, pyoderma gangrenosum, and acne (PAPA) syndrome
next_steps: Perform a punch biopsy of the advancing edge of an ulcer for histopathology (neutrophilic infiltrate) and tissue culture to rule out infection
Initiate systemic corticosteroid therapy (e.g., high-dose IV methylprednisolone) as the primary treatment for pyoderma gangrenosum
Perform a colonoscopy or capsule endoscopy to evaluate for underlying inflammatory bowel disease
Consult dermatology and rheumatology for management of autoinflammatory spectrum disorders
Evaluate for specific genetic mutations associated with autoinflammatory syndromes (e.g., PSTPIP1)
Continue supportive care and reassess the need for broad-spectrum antibiotics given the failure of multiple regimens and likelihood of pathergy-induced lesions
